etymology.description: еҲҖ(еҲҖ) (dДҒo, "knife") + зі№(зіё) (sД«, "silk") + еҚӘ(еҚӘ) (phonetic) -> to cut silk threads -> became зө•
etymology.interpretation: The character рўҮҚ illustrates the act of cutting silk threads using a knife, with the phonetic component еҚӘ lending its pronunciation. Over time, this form evolved into зө•(зө•) (juГ©, "cut off") with the same conceptual meaning but different orthography.
